data_source_summary: PM-KISAN Central Notification (December 1, 2018); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26 (national-state coordination); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; pmkisan.gov.in portal

Q25.r: PM-KISAN scheme guidelines explicitly mandate an income output indicator: ₹6,000/year direct income support per landholding farmer household, disbursed in 3 instalments of ₹2,000 each. The scheme's primary objective is income generation — the Outcome Budget 2025-26 tracks total benefit disbursed (₹1,220 Cr), beneficiary count (32.4 lakh), and payment success rate (98.4%) as mandatory output indicators. The Economic Survey 2024-25 confirms the income pathway: 'Scheme helps farmers manage agricultural expenses and household needs.' Income support is both mandated and measured.
Q25.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification December 1, 2018; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

observed.calc: Scheme-mandated transfer = ₹6,000/HH/year (3 × ₹2,000 instalments). CAG-verified actual disbursement at 98.4% success rate across 32.4 lakh farmers. Total ₹1,220 Cr ÷ 32.4 lakh HH = ₹3,765 avg (partial year FY 2024-25); full-year entitlement = ₹6,000/HH/year confirmed by scheme notification and CAG reconciliation.
observed.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

projected.calc: Projected DPR estimate not applicable — PM-KISAN is an ongoing operational scheme with directly observed CAG-audited payment data. No separate DPR income projection exists.

Q26.r: Observed ΔY governs. CAG-audited and Outcome Budget-verified actual payment data exists (₹1,220 Cr distributed to 32.4 lakh farmers in FY 2024-25). Full-year entitlement of ₹6,000/HH/year is confirmed by scheme notification and reconciled fund flow records. No separate projected DPR estimate applies as scheme is operational with verified disbursement data.
docs: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — PM-KISAN fund flow audit (₹1,220 Cr, 98.4% success)
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — beneficiary statistics (32.4 lakh, ₹3,765 avg FY 2024-25 partial year)
Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 (scheme income support confirmation)

Q27.r: Single explicitly articulated livelihood pathway: direct cash income support for agricultural expenses and household needs. The Economic Survey 2024-25 states: 'PM-KISAN national scheme provides income support to farmer households... Scheme helps farmers manage agricultural expenses and household needs.' PM-KISAN scheme guidelines articulate only one pathway — DBT cash transfer to farmers. No other distinct livelihood pathways (irrigation-driven productivity, NTFP enterprise, cooperative income, value-chain integration, etc.) are articulated in scheme documents. The scheme provides a cash input but does not mandate or create any additional income-generating pathway beyond the transfer itself.
Q27.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification December 1, 2018; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q28.r: PM-KISAN is a pure cash transfer scheme. No institutional offtake mechanism for agricultural produce or services derived from farming activities is created, mandated, or linked to this scheme. The DBT payment channel delivers cash to farmer accounts but does not constitute a market or produce offtake mechanism. There is no MSP procurement, government agency offtake agreement (TDCCOL/NAFED/OMC), GI tag buyer network, cooperative marketing arrangement, MGNREGS wage, BMC agreement, or any other institutional demand channel for produce/services that PM-KISAN creates or mandates. Farmers must rely on unguaranteed open market for produce sales. Score = No → AUTOMATIC T3 FAIL.
Q28.src: PM-KISAN Official Notification December 1, 2018; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; pmkisan.gov.in

Q29.r: PM-KISAN tracks only monetary and administrative indicators: total benefit disbursed (₹Cr), beneficiary count, payment success rate (98.4%), rejection rate (0.8%), and database record currency (94% current). No non-monetary livelihood metrics — food sufficiency months, nutritional diversity, livestock feeding days, medicinal plant access, or food species count — are tracked under the scheme MIS or evaluation framework. Near-threshold flag does not apply: Observed ΔY (₹6,000) is well above the ₹3,000 threshold (2× the threshold), so potential under-measurement concern is not triggered.
Q29.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; pmkisan.gov.in portal (metrics tracked)

pass_verification.C1_T3.1: PASS — income output indicator (₹6,000/HH/year) is mandated in scheme guidelines and tracked in Outcome Budget
pass_verification.C2_deltaY: PASS — Observed ΔY = ₹6,000/HH/year ≥ ₹3,000/HH/year threshold; governing = Observed (CAG-audited)
pass_verification.C3_T3.3: PASS — T3.3 = 1 (single articulated pathway: direct cash income support) ≥ threshold of 1
pass_verification.C4_T3.4: FAIL — No institutional offtake mechanism for produce/services exists or is mandated; scheme is pure cash transfer with no market/procurement backstop
pass_verification.enh_T3.5_flag: ok — Observed ΔY (₹6,000) is 2× the pass threshold (₹3,000); near-threshold under-measurement concern does not apply
